title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves competitive quality on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, compared to competitors requiring four to five GPUs. Variants include 2B and 4B parameter versions optimized for mobile and edge deployment, and the license shifted from custom Gemma to Apache 2.0 to enable sovereign adoption across institutions in emerging markets.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma 4 and its case for ownership through open models. The headline claim: the 31B model reaches competitive quality on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, where competitors require four to five. The lineup adds 2B and 4B variants tuned for mobile and edge deployment, and the license moves from the custom Gemma terms to Apache 2.0, a shift aimed at sovereign adoption by institutions in emerging markets.
